topic.what: Most influential pre-modern medical encyclopedia, synthesizing Greco-Roman, Persian, and Indian knowledge into a systematic framework for stomach diseases.
topic.how: Temperament-based (mizaj) classification of gastric disorders with compound herbal remedies and systematic differential diagnosis.
topic.why_matters: Represents the most sophisticated pre-modern synthesis of cross-cultural gastric knowledge and was the standard medical text for 600+ years across Islamic and European universities.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Duration of use as medical textbook
characteristics.vs_baseline: Longer than any other single medical text

good_for: Herbal lead identification from compound formulas

novelty: Most comprehensive pre-modern differential diagnosis of gastric disorders
First major cross-cultural medical synthesis applied to stomach disease
Temperament-based personalization anticipates modern precision medicine concepts
open_problems: Humoral framework has no modern biological correlate
Compound remedies difficult to evaluate with modern methodology
